behavior: Ground-dwelling and chiefly a walking forager; often travels in coveys, runs between dense cover, and flushes in a short burst of rapid wingbeats when disturbed.
field_marks: Small, plump quail with a rounded body, small head, and very short tail
Male has a bold black-and-white striped face with a white throat and eyebrow
Female has a buff throat and buff eyebrow rather than the male's white facial pattern
Rich chestnut to rufous upperparts, finely mottled with black, buff, and white
Underparts show dense pale scaling and dark barring
Short, dark, slightly downcurved bill; gray legs and rounded wings
habitat: Semi-open country with a grassy ground layer and dense low cover: overgrown fields, grasslands, shrubby areas, woodland edges, and open pine or mixed woodland.

sources.title: Northern Bobwhite Identification — All About Birds
